message:
feat(runtime): extract RuntimeHostServices seam to make the browser startup blocker explicit

RuntimeBuilder now delegates worker thread spawning and deadline monitor
startup to a RuntimeHostServices trait, replacing the previous inline
std::thread code. This makes the remaining Rust-authored browser
bootstrap blocker an explicit missing trait implementation rather than
an implicit assumption scattered through the builder code.

Key types:
- RuntimeHostServices trait: spawn_workers() and start_deadline_monitor()
  with a kind() discriminant and browser_contract() accessor
- RuntimeHostServicesKind: NativeStdThread | BrowserHost enum for
  diagnostics and fail-closed path selection
- BrowserHostServicesContract: pins the V1 browser requirements
  (host-turn wakeups, worker bootstrap hooks, timer/deadline driving,
  lane-health callbacks for threadless startup)
- NativeThreadHostServices: the only shipped implementation, which
  extracts the previous inline spawn_worker_threads and
  start_deadline_monitor into standalone methods
- DeadlineMonitorHostService: wraps the shutdown flag + thread handle,
  with a disabled() constructor for configurations without monitoring

The builder now instantiates NativeThreadHostServices by default and
calls through the trait for all startup operations. The public API is
unchanged — no browser builder path is exposed. The module docs are
updated to describe the seam without overstating browser readiness.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
